Notice Of Motion To Compel Richard Mackota To Answer Special Interrogatories & For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-12-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Michelle McGill, appeared for Petitioner/Moving party
Thomas C. Chow, appeared for Respondent/Opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative rulings adopted on (1) Petitioner Fred Mackota's Motion To Compel Richard Mackota To Answer Special Interrogatories, Set One, is granted as to Nos. 7 and 10. The objections are overruled. The motion is granted as to Nos. 1, 18, 19, and 24. The responses are inadequate and incomplete. The objections are overruled.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 33, 34, and 35. The objections are sustained. Sanctions are denied.
(2) Petitioner Fred Mackota's Motion To Compel Richard Mackota To Answer Form Interrogatories, Set Two, is granted as to Form Interrogatory Nos. 12.1-12.7. Respondent is ordered to respond further within 10 days of notice of this order. Respondent's responses and opposition were a misuse of the discovery process. Petitioner's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $515, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.

Same Order made as to a second motion to compel answers to form interrogatories except further sanctions denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Respindents' First Set Of Special Interrogatories And First Set Of Requests For Documents, And Imposing Monetary Sanctions.
Off Calendar Apr-10-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
MICHELE L. MCGILL, ESQ. FOR PETITIONERS/MOVING PARTIES, RICHARD MACKOTA, STACEY MACKOTA
THOMAS CHOW, ESQ. FOR RESPONDENT/OPPOSING PARTY

TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Respondents Richard Mackota, Stacey Mackota's Motion To Compel Responses To First Set Of Special Interrogatories and First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents is granted. Petitioner's objections are overruled. The court finds that the objections and opposition were made without substantial justification. Sanctions are awarded against Petitioner in the amount of $875, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Mar-24-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Michelle L. McGill, appeared for Petitioner/Moving party
Thomas G. Chow, appeared for Respondent/Opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing Held and Tentative Ruling adopted: Petitioner Fred Mackota's Motion For Protective Order Terminating Or Limiting Deposition is granted as it seeks an order terminating the deposition. The court finds that there is no good cause to order the continuation of Petitioner's deposition. Respondent has had adequate opportunity to depose Petitioner and has not shown the need for additional testimony or demonstrated what relevant testimony he was unable to obtain. COMM: EVERETT A.
